steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what both authors would agree on. Both Text 1 and Text 2 must directly support the correct answer — not just one text, and not something either text merely implies without stating.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: neither text argues that streaming services actively encourage longer albums — they describe a trend, not a platform directive. ✗
  • Choice B: neither text claims artists are padding albums with low-quality tracks. ✗
  • Choice C — 'new albums are longer than they once were': Text 1 states artists are "making albums that are much longer than albums from decades past"; Text 2 states "many new albums are longer and have more songs than albums crafted for physical formats." Both texts confirm this directly. ✓
  • Choice D: neither text claims listeners prefer shorter albums — the texts describe what artists are producing, not listener preference. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
